content.claim: Keeping multiple small village schools open historically limited within-school segregation but did not prevent between-settlement segregation, while territorial concentration of Roma population is increasing ghetto schools primarily in villages, with about half located in North-East Hungary
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Historical pattern may not hold under current centralized governance structure

Segregation mechanism (white flight to nearby towns in villages versus within-school sorting in cities) is documented but whether closure relocates versus reduces sorting is an inference, not directly tested
Regional concentration in North-East Hungary noted but causal factors not fully specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
